pattern: Count mismatched position pairs by type ('x' vs 'y' and 'y' vs 'x'); two same-type mismatches resolve in one swap, two different-type mismatches need two swaps.
hints: Only positions where s1[i] != s2[i] matter; collect them by which character each string has at that position.
Positions where s1='x', s2='y' pair up to resolve in one swap each; similarly for s1='y', s2='x'.
If the two mismatch types have different parities, you need an extra swap (converting one type into the other at cost 2); if parities are different you also check feasibility.

edge_inputs.description: two xy and two yx mismatches
cross mismatch odd count requires one extra swap
mixed types needing combination of single and double swaps

stress.description: random strings of x and y with equal character counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(1000, max(1, size))
    # ensure total x count is even so equal strings can exist
    xs = rng.randint(0, n)
    ys = n - xs
    chars = ['x'] * xs + ['y'] * ys
    rng.shuffle(chars)
    s1 = ''.join(chars[:n//2 * 2 // 2]) if n >= 2 else 'x'
    # simpler: build two strings with same char counts
    half = n // 2
    xs2 = rng.randint(0, half)
    ys2 = half - xs2
    c1 = ['x'] * xs2 + ['y'] * ys2
    c2 = ['x'] * xs2 + ['y'] * ys2
    rng.shuffle(c1)
    rng.shuffle(c2)
    return [''.join(c1), ''.join(c2)]
